title: Methotrexate: dihydrofolate polyglutamate
vignette: A 23-year-old woman comes to the emergency department with vaginal bleeding and lower abdominal cramps. Her last menstrual period was 6 weeks ago, and a home pregnancy test was positive. She has no medical history and does not use tobacco or alcohol. Pelvic ultrasonography reveals a gestational sac in the left fallopian tube without evidence of rupture. A single dose of methotrexate is administered to treat her condition. Which of the following substances will most likely accumulate in embryonic tissues as a result of treatment?

clues.text: vaginal bleeding and lower abdominal cramps
clues.note: Early-pregnancy bleeding with cramping raises concern for an abnormal (ectopic) pregnancy, setting up the indication for methotrexate.

clues.text: last menstrual period was 6 weeks ago, and a home pregnancy test was positive
clues.note: A positive pregnancy test at ~6 weeks confirms an early pregnancy. The question is where that pregnancy is implanted.

clues.text: gestational sac in the left fallopian tube without evidence of rupture
clues.note: A gestational sac in the fallopian tube = ectopic pregnancy; 'without rupture' makes it a candidate for medical (non-surgical) management with methotrexate.
Single-dose methotrexate treats an early unruptured ectopic by halting rapidly dividing trophoblast. The question tests its mechanism on the folate pathway.

whatMatters: The clinical wrapper just establishes 'early unruptured ectopic -> methotrexate.' The testable point is MTX's biochemistry: it inhibits dihydrofolate (DHF) reductase, so the SUBSTRATE upstream of the blocked enzyme accumulates while the product falls. DHF builds up (trapped as dihydrofolate POLYGLUTAMATE); tetrahydrofolate and downstream DNA precursors fall. Answer A.
strategy: For any enzyme-inhibitor question, ask which side of the block you're on: the SUBSTRATE accumulates, the PRODUCT decreases. MTX blocks DHF reductase (DHF -> THF). So DHF rises (as polyglutamate, because folates are trapped intracellularly by polyglutamation) and THF/thymidylate/DNA synthesis fall.

eliminations.label: folinic acid — the rescue you GIVE, not what accumulates
eliminations.detail: WHAT IT IS: leucovorin, a reduced folate that bypasses DHF reductase and 'rescues' normal cells from methotrexate toxicity. ❌ WHY IT FAILS: it's an antidote you administer, not a substance that builds up from MTX; it would be consumed, not accumulate. ✅ Correct if: asked what reverses MTX toxicity (leucovorin rescue).

eliminations.label: PABA — a bacterial folate precursor
eliminations.detail: WHAT IT IS: para-aminobenzoic acid is a folate precursor in BACTERIA; sulfonamides block its conversion (dihydropteroate synthase). ❌ WHY IT FAILS: humans don't synthesize folate from PABA, and MTX acts at DHF reductase, not the PABA step. ✅ Correct if: a sulfonamide/antibacterial folate-synthesis question.

eliminations.label: tetrahydrofolate — DECREASES with MTX (your pick)
eliminations.detail: WHAT IT IS: THF is the active reduced folate (the PRODUCT of DHF reductase) needed for one-carbon transfers and DNA synthesis. ❌ WHY IT FAILS: MTX BLOCKS DHF reductase, so THF formation FALLS — it doesn't accumulate; the upstream substrate (DHF) builds up instead. The trap is grabbing the famous active folate. ✅ Correct if: asked what DECREASES with MTX (THF/DNA synthesis).

eliminations.label: thymidylic acid — its synthesis falls
eliminations.detail: WHAT IT IS: dTMP, a pyrimidine nucleotide made by thymidylate synthase using a folate cofactor. ❌ WHY IT FAILS: with less folate cofactor, dTMP synthesis is impaired, so it falls rather than accumulates (the basis of the antiproliferative effect). ✅ Correct if: asked which nucleotide DROPS with MTX/5-FU.

reasoning: Methotrexate competitively and irreversibly inhibits dihydrofolate (DHF) reductase
Blocking DHF reductase stops conversion of DHF -> tetrahydrofolate, so the SUBSTRATE upstream (DHF) accumulates while products fall
Inside cells, folates are trapped by polyglutamation, so DHF builds up specifically as dihydrofolate POLYGLUTAMATE; downstream THF, thymidylate, and DNA synthesis decrease
mechanism: Methotrexate is a folate analog that competitively and irreversibly inhibits dihydrofolate reductase, the enzyme that reduces dihydrofolate (DHF) to tetrahydrofolate (THF). With the enzyme blocked, THF — and downstream thymidylate and purines for DNA synthesis — falls, while the upstream substrate DHF accumulates. After entering cells, methotrexate and folates undergo polyglutamation, which traps them intracellularly; thus DHF accumulates specifically as dihydrofolate polyglutamate. The antiproliferative effect is strongest in rapidly dividing tissue such as a developing embryo, which is why single-dose methotrexate treats an early unruptured ectopic pregnancy; leucovorin (folinic acid) can rescue normal cells from toxicity.

difficulty.label: 📚 Pure knowledge gap
difficulty.message: First Aid Pharm/Biochem > folate pathway and antifolates: DHF reductase (methotrexate, trimethoprim, pyrimethamine), thymidylate synthase (5-FU), dihydropteroate synthase (sulfonamides, PABA step); leucovorin rescue; intracellular polyglutamation.

difficulty.label: 🧭 Understanding the question
difficulty.message: Trace the pathway and ask which side of the BLOCKED enzyme you're on. MTX blocks DHF reductase -> product (THF) down, SUBSTRATE (DHF) up -> DHF accumulates (as polyglutamate).

difficulty.label: 🔤 Vocabulary
difficulty.message: Unsure on DHF reductase, tetrahydrofolate, polyglutamation, thymidylate synthase, leucovorin/folinic acid, PABA? Tell me which.

difficulty.label: 🧲 Pulled by a distractor
difficulty.message: Tetrahydrofolate (D) is the trap — it's the 'important' active folate, so it feels right; but MTX blocks its formation, so THF DROPS. What accumulates is the upstream DHF.

difficulty.label: ✍️ Something else
difficulty.message: Bank the rule: inhibit an enzyme -> substrate accumulates, product falls. MTX -> DHF up, THF down.

keywords.back: Reduced folate that rescues normal cells from MTX

firstAid.detail: Pharm > DHF reductase inhibition: THF falls, DHF (polyglutamate) accumulates; antiproliferative; leucovorin rescue.
firstAid.location: Pharm > DHF reductase inhibition: THF falls, DHF (polyglutamate) accumulates; antiproliferative; leucovorin rescue.

firstAid.detail: Biochem/Pharm > THF (active folate), thymidylate synthase/dTMP (5-FU target), PABA/dihydropteroate synthase (sulfonamides), leucovorin.
firstAid.location: Biochem/Pharm > THF (active folate), thymidylate synthase/dTMP (5-FU target), PABA/dihydropteroate synthase (sulfonamides), leucovorin.

firstAid.detail: Repro > unruptured tubal pregnancy: single-dose MTX criteria, beta-hCG follow-up, vs surgical salpingostomy/salpingectomy.
firstAid.location: Repro > unruptured tubal pregnancy: single-dose MTX criteria, beta-hCG follow-up, vs surgical salpingostomy/salpingectomy.

memoryHook: Block the enzyme -> the stuff BEFORE it piles up. MTX blocks DHF reductase -> DHF (polyglutamate) accumulates; THF crashes.
